---
title: "Protected & Conserved Areas"
description: "How much of Canada's land and water is protected, and where"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os, json
import pandas as pd
from pipeline.charts import (choropleth_map, choropleth_clean, lines_over_time, bubble_map,
                             CANADA_SMALL_PROV_OFFSET)

MAP_CONFIG = {"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True,
              "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]}
prov_geo = "data/geo/prov_2021_landmass.geojson"   # coastline-clipped: real islands
prov_csv = "data/geo/protected_areas_by_prov.csv"
nat_csv = "data/geo/protected_areas_national.csv"
```

## How much of Canada is protected

Canada has committed to conserving **30% of its land and ocean by 2030** — the international
"30 by 30" goal. As of 2024, about **14% of the country's land** is protected or under other
effective area-based conservation measures. But the share varies widely by jurisdiction: the
map below runs from around **5% in Prince Edward Island** to **21% in Yukon**, reflecting both
geography and differing provincial and territorial conservation efforts.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(prov_geo) and os.path.exists(prov_csv):
    g = json.load(open(prov_geo))
    d = pd.read_csv(prov_csv, dtype={"pruid": str})
    fig = choropleth_clean(g, d, "pruid", "pct_conserved", name_col="name",
        colorbar_title="% conserved", colorscale="Greens", value_suffix="%", value_fmt=".0f",
        label_offset=CANADA_SMALL_PROV_OFFSET,
        source_note="Source: ECCC, Canadian Environmental Sustainability Indicators — conserved areas (2024).")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Protected-areas data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

## Canada's national parks

Beyond the headline percentages, Parks Canada looks after more than 40 **national parks and park reserves**. Each circle below is a park, placed at its centre and sized by area — **zoom and pan to explore.** A handful of vast northern parks anchor the system (**Wood Buffalo**, larger than Switzerland, is the biggest), alongside small, heavily-visited southern parks like **Point Pelee** and the **Thousand Islands**.

```{python}
#| echo: false
parks_csv = "data/geo/national_parks.csv"
if os.path.exists(parks_csv):
    p = pd.read_csv(parks_csv)
    fig = bubble_map(p, "lat", "lon", "area_km2", color="#2e7d32", opacity=0.6, max_px=44,
        customdata=p[["name", "type", "area_km2"]].values,
        hovertemplate="<b>%{customdata[0]}</b><br>%{customdata[1]}<br>%{customdata[2]:,} km²<extra></extra>",
        center={"lat": 62, "lon": -96}, zoom=2.2,
        source_note="Source: Parks Canada, Places administered by Parks Canada (national parks & park reserves); area computed from the official boundaries. Non-legal boundaries.")
    fig.show(config=MAP_CONFIG)
else:
    print("National parks data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

➜ **[See every park's actual boundary](parks.qmd)** — a detailed map of more than 700 national, **provincial**, and territorial park *shapes*, coloured by who manages them.

## Progress toward "30 by 30"

The conserved share of Canada has roughly **tripled since 1990** — from about 4% of the land
to 14% — and the marine share has climbed faster still. Both, however, remain well short of
the 30% target now set for 2030.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(nat_csv):
    n = pd.read_csv(nat_csv)
    long = n.melt(id_vars="year", value_vars=["terrestrial_pct", "marine_pct"],
                  var_name="s", value_name="pct")
    long["s"] = long["s"].map({"terrestrial_pct": "Land", "marine_pct": "Ocean"})
    fig = lines_over_time(long, "year", "pct", "s", yaxis_title="Share conserved",
        yticksuffix="%", hover_decimals=1, colors={"Land": "#2ca25f", "Ocean": "#1f78b4"},
        source_note="Source: ECCC, Canadian Environmental Sustainability Indicators — conserved areas.")
    fig.add_hline(y=30, line_dash="dash", line_color="#555",
                  annotation_text="30% by 2030 target", annotation_position="top left")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Download data

- [Conserved area by province (CSV)](../data/geo/protected_areas_by_prov.csv) — ECCC CESI, 2024
- [Conserved area, Canada, 1990– (CSV)](../data/geo/protected_areas_national.csv) — ECCC CESI
- [National parks & park reserves (CSV)](../data/geo/national_parks.csv) — Parks Canada (centroid + area)
